item.title: Alpine email client
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Alpine is a softôÙware I defôÙiôÙniteôÙly recôÙomôÙmend for those who like minôÙiôÙmalôÙisôÙtic apôÙpliôÙcaôÙtionôÙs that go straight to the point in orôÙder to get the things done. It's an email clienôÙt, with no GUI, which alôÙlows me to read emails betôÙter than by means of the web page [1] withôÙout any disôÙtracôÙtionôÙs, foôÙcusôÙing on what is just imôÙporôÙtanôÙt. It is alôÙso great that shows onôÙly the text (the relôÙeôÙvant part of the email) and it can folôÙlow the links by launchôÙing the deôÙfault browsôÙer.

It is simôÙple, fast and perôÙfectôÙly suitôÙable for checkôÙing my old inôÙbox with weekôÙly lists upôÙdates. It's inôÙherôÙitôÙed from an old proôÙjecôÙt, called pine, and in FeôÙdoôÙra it can be inôÙstalled by:

sudo dnf install alpine

Then you just need to conôÙfigôÙure the email acôÙcount setôÙtings (one last time to visôÙit the page, but that would be the last :-) and you're ready.

[1]Not for gmail but from another older account I have
